sections.title: Performance Analysis — Distill (book_shorts)
sections.content: This is a static-content React SPA (no backend), so "N+1 queries" show up as **repeated linear scans / recomputation** instead of DB round-trips. Findings below, ranked by impact.

sections.title: 1. Unnecessary re-renders: every `BookCard` re-renders when *any* book's progress changes
2. `bookStats(book)` recomputed on every render, everywhere it's used
3. Linear scans standing in for indexed lookups ("N+1" pattern)
4. `searchBooks` does full-corpus work on every keystroke, no debounce
5. Chapter content re-parsed from markdown on every visit — missing cache
6. Unthrottled scroll handler forces a render on every scroll tick
7. `BookCard` isn't memoized — Library keystrokes re-render the whole grid
sections.content:
`src/pages/Library.tsx:92-231`

`query` state lives in `Library`. Every keystroke re-renders `Library`, and since `BookCard` isn't wrapped in `memo`, every card in every category track re-renders too — even though only the search-results section (which isn't shown until `searching` is true) actually depends on `query`. Combine with fix #1's selector hook:

```tsx
const BookCard = memo(function BookCard(...) { ... })
```

sections.title: Memory leaks / cleanup
sections.content: No real leaks found — `useEffect` cleanup for scroll/keydown listeners and the `alive` flag pattern in `Reader`'s chapter-load effect are both correct. The `store.ts` listener `Set` is properly pruned via the `unsubscribe` returned to `useSyncExternalStore`. One minor note: `chapterCache` (fix #5) should stay unbounded-but-small since chapter count is fixed content, not user-generated — no eviction needed.

sections.title: Priority order
sections.content:
1. Selector-based progress subscription + `memo(BookCard)` (#1, #7) — biggest win, fixes O(N) re-renders on every progress write.
2. Cache `loadChapter`/`mdToHtml` output (#5) — avoids re-parsing markdown on repeat visits.
3. Memoize `bookStats` (#2) and index `categoryOf`/`booksInCategory` (#3) — cheap, compounds with #1.
4. Throttle scroll handler (#6).
5. Debounce search (#4) — only matters once the catalog grows past ~50-100 books.

codeBlocks.code:
const deferredQuery = useDeferredValue(query)
const results = useMemo(() => searchBooks(deferredQuery), [deferredQuery])
